mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions signal robust consumer spending capacity, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and residential housing starts amplifies aggregate demand shocks, forcing equity markets to price in higher near-term cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline GDPC1-only model in out-of-sample backtests, or 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if INDPRO rises while SPX 20-day returns remain negative in more than 60% of late-cycle observations, indicating supply growth no longer translates to equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points within a single month signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a 0.25pp rate hike, or the negative return signal disappears after adjusting for CPIAUCSL inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in residential construction spending reflects sustained household confidence and credit availability, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling RSAFS periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable consumer price inflation confirms a non-inflationary labor market recovery, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy while corporate revenues expand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-CPIAUCSL signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when PAYEMS rises and CPIAUCSL remains flat, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 95 while housing starts remain above 1100 units signals a synchronized supply-side recovery meeting robust demand, which rapidly lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where both INDPRO and HOUST exceed their respective thresholds, or the signal shows no edge over a simple INDPRO-only momentum model.
